(2) DEFENDANTS BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC., AND HUGH VANDERSPEK'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 80 AS IT SEEKS PRODUCTION. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO IDENTIFY ANY DOCUMENTS WITHHELD PURSUANT TO A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE IN A PRIVILEGE LOG. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO NO. 88 AS THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. THE RESPONSE OTHERWISE MAY BE ADEQUATE. AS TO THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO NOS. 15, 18, 21, 7, AND 12 AS IT SEEKS THE IDENTIFICATION OF ANY WITNESS STATEMENT WITHOUT IDENTIFYING WHO TOOK THE STATEMENT. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO NOS. 17 AND 20. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 82, THE MOTION IS GRANTED. LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, DISQUALIFY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL DUE TO MISCONDUCT IS DENIED. "CALIFORNIA COURTS HAVE INHERENT POWER TO TERMINATE LITIGATION FOR DELIBERATE AND EGREGIOUS MISCONDUCT WHEN NO OTHER REMEDY CAN RESTORE FAIRNESS'" STEPHEN SLESINGER, INC. V. WALT DISNEY CO. (2007) 155 CAL.APP.4TH 736, 761. IN STEPHEN SLESINGER, INC., THE PLAINTIFF WHOSE CASE WAS DISMISSED ENGAGED IN CRIMINAL TRESPASS, STOLE CONFIDENTIAL DOCUMENTS, AND ALTERED DOCUMENTS WITH THE INTENT TO MISLEAD. THE ALLEGED MISCONDUCT OF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IN THIS CASE DOES NOT RISE TO THAT LEVEL AND DISMISSAL IS NOT WARRANTED. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION TO DISQUALIFY. "IF THE STATUS OR MISCONDUCT WHICH IS URGED AS A GROUND FOR DISQUALIFICATION WILL HAVE A CONTINUING EFFECT ON THE JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS WHICH ARE BEFORE THE COURT, IT IS JUSTIFIED IN REFUSING TO PERMIT THE LAWYER TO PARTICIPATE IN SUCH PROCEEDINGS." CHRONOMETRICS, INC. SYSGEN, INC. (1980) 110 CAL.APP.3D 597, 607. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS LARGELY BASED ON SPECULATION AND SUPPOSITION. THE COURT DOES NOT HAVE A BASIS TO KNOW WHETHER THE JURY POOL HAS REVIEWED THE CITED MATERIAL OR WHETHER JURORS HAVE BEEN TAINTED BEFORE A JURY PANEL HAS BEEN SUMMONED AND QUESTIONED. THE TRIAL JUDGE SUPERVISING VOIR DIRE WILL BE IN THE BEST POSITION TO DETERMINE IF THERE IS A PROBLEM AND, IF SO, HOW BEST TO ADDRESS IT. THE TRIAL JUDGE WILL ALSO BE ABLE TO ISSUE AND ENFORCE MOTIONS IN LIMINE THAT CAN CONTROL AND AVOID PREJUDICIAL CONDUCT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING RE: DEFENDANT BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC.'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND/OR ASKING THE COURT TO EXERCISE ITS INHERENT POWER TO CONTROL PROCEEDINGS BEFORE IT. DEFENDANTS HAVE AT MOST SHOWN A POSSIBILITY OF PREJUDICE, WHICH IS INSUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE THAT A PRIOR RESTRAINT IS WARRANTED IN THIS CASE. AS STATED IN THE ORDER DENYING DISMISSAL/DISQUALIFICATION, THE COURT DOES NOT NOW KNOW WHETHER THERE IS PREJUDICE CAUSED BY COUNSEL'S WEBSITES/STATEMENTS. "WHERE A PARTY CONTENDS HIS OR HER RIGHT TO A FAIR TRIAL HAS BEEN OR WILL BE COMPROMISED BY PRETRIAL PUBLICITY, THE LAW HAS LONG IMPOSED ON THAT PARTY THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH THE PREJUDICE. '[I]T IS NOT ENOUGH FOR A COURT TO DECIDE THAT THE FAIR TRIAL RIGHT MAY BE AFFECTED BY THE EXERCISE OF FREE SPEECH.'" HURVITZ V. HOEFFLIN (2001) 84 CAL.APP.4TH 1232, 1242. IT IS PREMATURE TO DETERMINE WHETHER THE JURY POOL HAS BEEN TAINTED. JUROR ADMONITIONS AND INSTRUCTIONS ARE PRESUMPTIVELY ADEQUATE MEANS TO ADDRESS THE THREAT OF JURY CONTAMINATION. STEINER V. SUP. CRT. (2013) 220 CAL.APP.4TH 1479, 1487. THE TRIAL JUDGE SUPERVISING VOIR DIRE WILL BE IN THE BEST POSITION TO DETERMINE IF THERE IS A PROBLEM AND, IF SO, HOW BEST TO ADDRESS IT. ALTHOUGH THE COURT IS NOT GRANTING THE REQUESTED RELIEF, THE COURT EXPECTS ALL PARTIES AND COUNSEL TO ABIDE BY THEIR LEGAL AND ETHICAL OBLIGATIONS BEFORE AND DURING TRIAL. AGAIN, THE TRIAL JUDGE WILL BE IN THE BEST POSITION TO DETERMINE WHAT IF ANY REDRESS MIGHT BE APPROPRIATE FOR ANY MISCONDUCT THAT MAY OCCUR. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Or In The Alternative, Disqualify Pltff'S Counsel Due To Misconduct
Off Calendar Jun-05-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Briana McCarthy, Esq., 310.424.3355, for plaintiff Randall Radford; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC's Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative, Disqualify Plaintiff's Counsel Due To Misconduct is denied. "California courts have inherent power to terminate litigation for deliberate and egregious misconduct when no other remedy can restore fairness'" Stephen Slesinger, Inc. v. Walt Disney Co. (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 736, 761. In Stephen Slesinger, Inc., the plaintiff whose case was dismissed engaged in criminal trespass, stole confidential documents, and altered documents with the intent to mislead. The alleged misconduct of plaintiff's counsel in this case does not rise to that level and dismissal is not warranted. The Court denies the motion to disqualify. "If the status or misconduct which is urged as a ground for disqualification will have a continuing effect on the judicial proceedings which are before the court, it is justified in refusing to permit the lawyer to participate in such proceedings." Chronometrics, Inc. Sysgen, Inc. (1980) 110 Cal.App.3d 597, 607. Defendant's motion is largely based on speculation and supposition. The Court does not have a basis to know whether the jury pool has reviewed the cited material or whether jurors have been tainted before a jury panel has been summoned and questioned. The trial judge supervising voir dire will be in the best position to determine if there is a problem and, if so, how best to address it. The trial judge will also be able to issue and enforce motions in limine that can control and avoid prejudicial conduct. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jun-05-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Briana McCarthy, Esq., 310.424.3355, for plaintiff Randall Radford; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANT BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC.'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. The Court denies defendants' motion for preliminary injunction and/or asking the Court to exercise its inherent power to control proceedings before it. Defendants have at most shown a possibility of prejudice, which is insufficient to demonstrate that a prior restraint is warranted in this case. As stated in the order denying dismissal/disqualification, the Court does not now know whether there is prejudice caused by counsel's websites/statements. "Where a party contends his or her right to a fair trial has been or will be compromised by pretrial publicity, the law has long imposed on that party the burden of producing evidence to establish the prejudice. '[I]t is not enough for a court to decide that the fair trial right may be affected by the exercise of free speech.'" Hurvitz v. Hoefflin (2001) 84 Cal.App.4th 1232, 1242. It is premature to determine whether the jury pool has been tainted. Juror admonitions and instructions are presumptively adequate means to address the threat of jury contamination. Steiner v. Sup. Crt. (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1479, 1487. The trial judge supervising voir dire will be in the best position to determine if there is a problem and, if so, how best to address it. Although the Court is not granting the requested relief, the Court expects all parties and counsel to abide by their legal and ethical obligations before and during trial. Again, the trial judge will be in the best position to determine what if any redress might be appropriate for any misconduct that may occur. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF RANDALL RADFORD's Motion To Consolidate Actions For Trial Pursuant To Ccp 1048(A) is denied. The cases do not share sufficient common facts or law to warrant consolidation. Further, granting consolidation and having the trial for all actions on May 19, 2014, would prejudice defendant because expert discovery has yet to occur in the Foster action. MINI MINUTES: MINUTE ORDER DENYING MOTION FOR PARTIAL JUDGMENT NOTWITHSTANDING THE VERDICT AND CONDITIONALLY GRANTING MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL

Motion for Partial Judgment Notwithstanding the Verdict is DENIED. The Court denies the motion because there was substantial evidence, or reasonable inferences to be drawn therefrom, to support the jury's verdict that defendant BAE Systems San Francisco Ship Repair, Inc. acted with malice, oppression or fraud in terminating Plaintiff Randall Radford, and in failing to prevent this racial discrimination.

Motion for New Trial, or in the alternative Remittitur, is GRANTED on the following grounds:

(1) Irregularity in the proceedings of the Court, jury or plaintiff or orders of the Court of abuse of discretion by which the defendant BAE Systems San Francisco Ship Repair, Inc. was prevented from having a fair trial (CCP 657(1));

(2) Excessive damages (CCP 657(5)); and

(3) Error in law occurring at trial and excepted to by defendant BAE Systems San Francisco Ship Repair, Inc. (CCP 657(7)).

The order granting the new trial is subject to the Plaintiff accepting a remittiur of $55,455 in past economic damages, $166,365 in past noneconomic damages, and $250,000 in punitive damages within 15 days of the date of this order.

Pursuant to CCP 657, the specifications of reasons supporting the above orders will be filed and served within 10 days.
